text: The nitrate reduction reaction (NO3RR) for the electrochemical synthesis of ammonia using ammonia synthesis using the Haber-Bosch process is attractive but the process is not very efficient in concentrated electrolytes. While many high performance catalysts, like Cu2O, can yield high activity and ammonia selectivity in diluted solutions, a high nitrate concentration leads to surface poisoning caused by adsorption of nitrate species on the catalyst surface that block the hydrogen acquisition sites required for further hydrogenation. To alleviate this kinetically mismatched effect, we prepared ruthenium clusters on Cu2O nanowires (Ru-Cu2O). Ru-domains act as catalytic hot spots that facilitate the hydrogen adsorption and ammonia synthesis with sufficient reaction kinetics in nitrate concentrated electrolyte solutions. Under 1 M nitrate solution, Ru-Cu2O catalysts can retain ~100% Faradaic efficiency towards ammonia production. An ammonia flow electrolyzer using Ru-Cu2O as catalyst achieves a full-cell current of 10.0 A at 16 cm² electrodes and corresponding ammonia price is 6.87 USD/kg. Using a combination of deuterium labelling experiments, operando Fourier-transform infrared spectroscopy, and ab-initio molecular dynamics simulations, the positive effect of Ru on the Cu2O catalyst was demonstrated by identifying the proton-coupled electron transfer process and its corresponding energy barriers that are significantly decreased in the Ru-Cu2O catalysts. Overall, the present work exemplifies a new concept to optimize catalytic performance in the process of co-catalysts in various solutions from dilute to concentrated.
